text:
Once upon a quiet town lived a boy named Theodore. Theodore lived in a house beside an ancient tree. The tree was wise. It possessed secrets and knew many things. 

One foggy afternoon, Theodore sat beneath the tree listening to its tales. The tree spoke of a mythical creature called the Whispering Sprite. The Sprite was said to glide through the forest at night, bringing dreams to restless minds.

Feeling inspired, Theodore decided to create something for people to dream about. He spent nights sketching visions of adventures. One night, he captured a Sprite—a graceful being with wings like silver feathers and eyes that sparkled like tiny stars.

The next morning, Theodore unveiled his masterpiece. He called it "The Whispering Dreams Book," complete with illustrations matching his imagined Sprite. The townspeople were captivated. Children dreamed of traveling enchanted forests, while adults fancied noble quests.

But one lonely afternoon, as Theodore sat beneath the ancient tree pondering his dream, he saw something extraordinary. From
